modules.title: Stage 1 · SQL Foundations
modules.description: Read tables, select columns and filter rows confidently.

lessons.title: SELECT and FROM
lessons.starter: SELECT order_id, customer, revenue FROM data LIMIT 5;
lessons.solution: SELECT order_id, customer, revenue FROM data LIMIT 5;
lessons.challenge: Select order_id, customer and revenue, returning only five rows.

lessons.title: Filtering with WHERE
lessons.description: Use conditions to find relevant records.
lessons.starter: SELECT * FROM data WHERE revenue >= 500;
lessons.solution: SELECT * FROM data WHERE revenue >= 500;
lessons.challenge: Return every order with revenue of at least 500.

lessons.title: ORDER BY and LIMIT
lessons.description: Sort results and control result size.
lessons.starter: SELECT customer, revenue FROM data ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 3;
lessons.solution: SELECT customer, revenue FROM data ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 3;
lessons.challenge: Return the three highest-revenue customers.

lessons.title: NULL and DISTINCT
lessons.description: Handle missing values and unique categories.
lessons.starter: SELECT DISTINCT region FROM data WHERE region IS NOT NULL ORDER BY region;
lessons.solution: SELECT DISTINCT region FROM data WHERE region IS NOT NULL ORDER BY region;
lessons.challenge: Return unique non-null regions in alphabetical order.

modules.title: Stage 2 · Analytical SQL
modules.description: Aggregate data and express business rules.

lessons.title: COUNT, SUM and AVG
lessons.starter: SELECT COUNT(*) AS orders, ROUND(SUM(revenue),2) AS total_revenue, ROUND(AVG(revenue),2) AS avg_revenue FROM data;
lessons.solution: SELECT COUNT(*) AS orders, ROUND(SUM(revenue),2) AS total_revenue, ROUND(AVG(revenue),2) AS avg_revenue FROM data;
lessons.challenge: Calculate order count, total revenue and average revenue.

lessons.title: GROUP BY
lessons.starter: SELECT region, ROUND(SUM(revenue),2) AS revenue FROM data GROUP BY region ORDER BY revenue DESC;
lessons.solution: SELECT region, ROUND(SUM(revenue),2) AS revenue FROM data GROUP BY region ORDER BY revenue DESC;
lessons.challenge: Calculate total revenue by region, highest first.

lessons.title: HAVING
lessons.starter: SELECT region, COUNT(*) AS orders FROM data GROUP BY region HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY orders DESC;
lessons.solution: SELECT region, COUNT(*) AS orders FROM data GROUP BY region HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY orders DESC;
lessons.challenge: Return regions with at least two orders.

lessons.title: CASE expressions
lessons.starter: SELECT order_id, revenue, CASE WHEN revenue >= 700 THEN 'High' WHEN revenue >= 400 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END AS revenue_band FROM data ORDER BY order_id;
lessons.solution: SELECT order_id, revenue, CASE WHEN revenue >= 700 THEN 'High' WHEN revenue >= 400 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END AS revenue_band FROM data ORDER BY order_id;
lessons.challenge: Classify each order into High, Medium or Low revenue.

modules.title: Stage 3 · Relational SQL
modules.description: Combine tables and reason about relationships.

lessons.title: INNER JOIN
lessons.starter: SELECT o.order_id, c.customer_name, o.revenue FROM orders o INNER JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id ORDER BY o.order_id;
lessons.solution: SELECT o.order_id, c.customer_name, o.revenue FROM orders o INNER JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id ORDER BY o.order_id;
lessons.challenge: Join orders to customers and show order id, customer name and revenue.

lessons.title: LEFT JOIN
lessons.starter: SELECT c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.customer_name ORDER BY c.customer_name;
lessons.solution: SELECT c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.customer_name ORDER BY c.customer_name;
lessons.challenge: Show every customer and their order count, including zero.

lessons.title: Multiple joins
lessons.starter: SELECT o.order_id, c.customer_name, p.product_name, oi.quantity FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id=o.order_id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id=p.product_id ORDER BY o.order_id, p.product_name;
lessons.solution: SELECT o.order_id, c.customer_name, p.product_name, oi.quantity FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id=o.order_id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id=p.product_id ORDER BY o.order_id, p.product_name;
lessons.challenge: Combine order items, orders, customers and products.

lessons.title: Subqueries
lessons.description: Use one query to inform another.
lessons.starter: SELECT customer, revenue FROM data WHERE revenue > (SELECT AVG(revenue) FROM data) ORDER BY revenue DESC;
lessons.solution: SELECT customer, revenue FROM data WHERE revenue > (SELECT AVG(revenue) FROM data) ORDER BY revenue DESC;
lessons.challenge: Return orders above average revenue.

modules.title: Stage 4 · Advanced SQL
modules.description: Use CTEs and window functions for deeper analysis.

lessons.title: Common table expressions
lessons.description: Break complex analysis into understandable steps.
lessons.starter: WITH regional AS (SELECT region, SUM(revenue) AS revenue FROM data GROUP BY region) SELECT region, ROUND(revenue,2) AS revenue FROM regional ORDER BY revenue DESC;
lessons.solution: WITH regional AS (SELECT region, SUM(revenue) AS revenue FROM data GROUP BY region) SELECT region, ROUND(revenue,2) AS revenue FROM regional ORDER BY revenue DESC;
lessons.challenge: Use a CTE to calculate and rank regional revenue.

lessons.title: ROW_NUMBER and RANK
lessons.starter: SELECT region, customer, revenue, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY revenue DESC) AS region_rank FROM data ORDER BY region, region_rank;
lessons.solution: SELECT region, customer, revenue, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY revenue DESC) AS region_rank FROM data ORDER BY region, region_rank;
lessons.challenge: Rank orders by revenue within each region.

lessons.title: Running totals
lessons.starter: SELECT order_date, revenue, ROUND(SUM(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY order_date, order_id),2) AS running_revenue FROM data ORDER BY order_date, order_id;
lessons.solution: SELECT order_date, revenue, ROUND(SUM(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY order_date, order_id),2) AS running_revenue FROM data ORDER BY order_date, order_id;
lessons.challenge: Calculate cumulative revenue by order date.

lessons.title: LAG and change analysis
lessons.description: Compare a row with an earlier row.
lessons.starter: WITH daily AS (SELECT order_date, SUM(revenue) AS revenue FROM data GROUP BY order_date) SELECT order_date, ROUND(revenue,2) AS revenue, ROUND(revenue-LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY order_date),2) AS change_from_previous FROM daily ORDER BY order_date;
lessons.solution: WITH daily AS (SELECT order_date, SUM(revenue) AS revenue FROM data GROUP BY order_date) SELECT order_date, ROUND(revenue,2) AS revenue, ROUND(revenue-LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY order_date),2) AS change_from_previous FROM daily ORDER BY order_date;
lessons.challenge: Calculate daily revenue and change from the previous date.

modules.title: Stage 5 · Database Engineering
modules.description: Design reliable schemas and reason about performance and controls.

lessons.title: Relational modelling
lessons.starter: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' ORDER BY name;
lessons.solution: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' ORDER BY name;
lessons.challenge: Inspect the available table definitions.

lessons.title: Keys and constraints
lessons.starter: PRAGMA foreign_key_list(order_items);
lessons.solution: PRAGMA foreign_key_list(order_items);
lessons.challenge: Inspect the foreign keys on order_items.

lessons.title: Indexes and query plans
lessons.starter: EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 2;
lessons.solution: EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 2;
lessons.challenge: Inspect the query plan for filtering orders by customer.

lessons.title: Transactions and consistency
lessons.starter: SELECT 'BEGIN → validate → write → COMMIT, otherwise ROLLBACK' AS transaction_pattern;
lessons.solution: SELECT 'BEGIN → validate → write → COMMIT, otherwise ROLLBACK' AS transaction_pattern;
lessons.challenge: Return a concise transaction pattern describing commit and rollback.
